technology.title: New cell imaging method shines a light on blind spots
technology.desc: Cells are crowded, dynamic places where thousands of molecules interact in tight quarters. Until now, scientists lacked a reliable way to see many of these molecular interactions as they happen. Researchers at the University of Illinois Chicago have now developed a new imaging method that allows scientists to see previously hidden enzyme activities in small regions across the whole cell. The findings, published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, open new possibilities for understanding how cells process information.

technology.desc: Apple Back to School 2026（BTS）開學優惠預計本週正式上線。根據 Bloomberg 記者 Mark Gurman 於 7 月 12 日在 X 平台發帖確認，Apple 零售店已開始佈置 BTS 宣傳物料，並重組陳列專設教育桌展示 Mac 及 iPad 系列，顯示推廣活動即將啟動。今年活動延遲的背景是 Apple 於 6 月 25 日因 DRAM 記憶體短缺而上調全線 Mac 及 iPad 售價，幅度達 6% 至 13%，BTS 贈品優惠因此備受關注。合資格用戶包括香港認可大專院校學生、教職員及家長，購買指定 Mac 可免費獲贈 AirPods 4 / AirPods Pro 3，購買 iPad Pro / iPad Air 可獲贈 Apple Pencil Pro，堪稱一年之中入手 Apple 裝備的最佳時機。

technology.title: I tried the Asus AR glasses with the OLED Ally - they felt extraneous.
technology.desc:
[Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/verge-ally-x20-sean-hollister-1.jpg?quality=90&strip=all]
I enjoy plugging Xreal glasses into handhelds for a huge virtual desktop — but that’s no reason to make us buy the priciest version with a 240Hz mode handhelds have trouble powering. Plus, 240Hz halves resolution from 1080p to 540p, which felt weird and blurry in person. (120Hz was fine.) Good thing Asus will now sell the OLED Ally separately!

world.title: What Zelensky wants from his next government
world.desc:
President Volodymyr Zelensky's decision on July 12 to reshuffle the government came as a surprise to many — both on the streets and in the halls of parliament.
According to the Constitution, the president cannot single-handedly dismiss the prime minister. Yet lawmakers who spoke with the Kyiv Independent appeared largely unfazed by Zelensky's move to replace Prime Minister Yuliia Svyrydenko, which parliament rubber-stamped on July 14.
